ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for the country */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language(s) spoken in the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "continents" (
  "continent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each continent */,
  "continent_name" TEXT /* Name of the continent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("continent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "covid_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country the data pertains to */,
  "total_cases" INTEGER /* Total number of confirmed cases */,
  "new_cases" INTEGER /* Number of new cases */,
  "total_deaths" INTEGER /* Total number of deaths */,
  "new_deaths" REAL /* Number of new deaths */,
  "total_recovered" INTEGER /* Total number of recoveries */,
  "new_recovered" INTEGER /* Number of new recoveries */,
  "active_cases" INTEGER /* Number of active cases */,
  "serious_critical" INTEGER /* Number of serious/critical cases */,
  "cases_per_million" REAL /* Total cases per 1 million population */,
  "deaths_per_million" REAL /* Total deaths per 1 million population */,
  "total_tests" INTEGER /* Total number of tests conducted */,
  "tests_per_million" REAL /* Tests conducted per 1 million population */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the data was collected */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_data" (
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country the population data pertains to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the population data is recorded */,
  "urban_population" INTEGER /* Urban population of the country */,
  "rural_population" INTEGER /* Rural population of the country */,
  "male_population" INTEGER /* Male population of the country */,
  "female_population" INTEGER /* Female population of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_data" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country the test data pertains to */,
  "total_tests" INTEGER /* Total number of tests conducted */,
  "tests_per_million" REAL /* Tests conducted per 1 million population */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the test data is recorded */,
  "positive_rate" REAL /* Positive rate of the tests */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of tests conducted (e.g., PCR, antigen) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date when the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who accessed the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data that was accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_description" TEXT /* Description of the access (e.g., what data was accessed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES covid_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each case report */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the COVID-19 data the report pertains to */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was created */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the case report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of case report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_case_reports_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES covid_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "death_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each death report */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the COVID-19 data the report pertains to */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was created */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the death report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of death report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_death_reports_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES covid_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vaccination_data" (
  "vaccination_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vaccination data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country the vaccination data pertains to */,
  "total_vaccinations" INTEGER /* Total number of vaccinations administered */,
  "people_vaccinated" INTEGER /* Number of people who have received at least one dose */,
  "people_fully_vaccinated" INTEGER /* Number of people who are fully vaccinated */,
  "vaccination_rate" REAL /* Vaccination rate (percentage of the population vaccinated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vaccination_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vaccination_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hospitalization_data" (
  "hospitalization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hospitalization data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country the hospitalization data pertains to */,
  "total_hospitalizations" INTEGER /* Total number of hospitalizations due to COVID-19 */,
  "icu_admissions" INTEGER /* Number of ICU admissions */,
  "ventilator_usage" INTEGER /* Number of ventilators in use */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hospitalization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hospitalization_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)
